mechanism: hash-based sparse Fourier localization; hidden-number Fourier/lattice decoding; lattice reduction interface; random-example noisy character learning; random-sample Fourier access separation; significant Fourier coefficient learning
no_go_barrier: Since we have good evidence that LWE is a hard problem, this shows that we do not expect to be able to learn significant Fourier coefficients in the random access model.
open_question: In particular, we point out that some of the most important bit security problems are still open.
problem_family: LPN/LWE-style learning problems; hidden number problems; lattice problems; random-access noisy Fourier learning; sparse Fourier recovery
proof_technique: In particular we provide a new proof of a result by Morillo and R \` a fols~ MR (described in Section~ sec:i-th-bit ).
reduction: The SFT algorithm has also been used to show search-to-decision reductions for the learning with errors and learning with rounding problems~ MM,LWR reduction .
reusable_abstraction: Akavia, Goldwasser and Safra unified some of these ideas to give a complete algorithm that finds significant Fourier coefficients of functions on any finite abelian group.

theorem_like_statements.label: definition-1
theorem_like_statements.raw_latex:
[Restriction]
    Given a function $f:G \rightarrow \C$ and a set of characters $\Gamma \subseteq \widehat{G}$, the \emph{restriction of $f$ to $\Gamma$} is the function $f|_\Gamma: G \rightarrow \C$ defined by $f|_\Gamma := \sum_{\chi_\alpha \in \Gamma}\widehat{f}(\alpha) \chi_\alpha$.
theorem_like_statements.statement: [Restriction] Given a function $f:G \rightarrow \C$ and a set of characters $\Gamma \subseteq \widehat{G}$, the \emph{restriction of $f$ to $\Gamma$} is the function $f|_\Gamma: G \rightarrow \C$ defined by $f|_\Gamma := \sum_{\chi_\alpha \in \Gamma}\widehat{f}(\alpha) \chi_\alpha$.

Let $\epsilon>0$ be a real number. %Then $\famly$ is \emph{Fourier $\epsilon$-concentrated} if there exist a polynomial $P \in \Z[s,t]$ and sets of characters $\Gamma_i \subseteq \widehat{G}_i$ such that $|\Gamma_i| \le P\Bigl( \tfrac{1}{\epsilon} , \log|G_i| \Big)$ and $\| f_i - f_i|_{\Gamma_i} \|_{2}^{2} \leq \epsilon$ for all $i\in \N$. theorem_like_statements.label: definition-3
theorem_like_statements.raw_latex:
[$\epsilon$-Concentration]
    Let $\epsilon>0$ be a real number. A family of functions $\{f_i:G_i\to \C\}_{i\in \N}$ is \emph{Fourier $\epsilon$-concentrated} if there exists a polynomial $P$ and sets of characters $\Gamma_i \subseteq \widehat{G}_i$ such that $|\Gamma_i| \le P( \log|G_i| )$ and $\| f_i - f_i|_{\Gamma_i} \|_{2}^{2} \leq \epsilon$ for all $i\in \N$.
theorem_like_statements.statement: [$\epsilon$-Concentration] Let $\epsilon>0$ be a real number. A family of functions $\{f_i:G_i\to \C\}_{i\in \N}$ is \emph{Fourier $\epsilon$-concentrated} if there exists a polynomial $P$ and sets of characters $\Gamma_i \subseteq \widehat{G}_i$ such that $|\Gamma_i| \le P( \log|G_i| )$ and $\| f_i - f_i|_{\Gamma_i} \|_{2}^{2} \leq \epsilon$ for all $i\in \N$.

theorem_like_statements.label: definition-4
theorem_like_statements.raw_latex:
[Concentration]
    A family of functions $\{f_i:G_i\to \C\}_{i\in \N}$ is \emph{Fourier concentrated} if there exists a polynomial $P$ and sets of characters $\Gamma_i \subseteq \widehat{G}_i$ such that $|\Gamma_i| \le P( \log|G_i|/\epsilon )$ and $\| f_i - f_i|_{\Gamma_i} \|_{2}^{2} \leq \epsilon$ for all $i\in \N$ and for all $\epsilon > 0$.
theorem_like_statements.statement: [Concentration] A family of functions $\{f_i:G_i\to \C\}_{i\in \N}$ is \emph{Fourier concentrated} if there exists a polynomial $P$ and sets of characters $\Gamma_i \subseteq \widehat{G}_i$ such that $|\Gamma_i| \le P( \log|G_i|/\epsilon )$ and $\| f_i - f_i|_{\Gamma_i} \|_{2}^{2} \leq \epsilon$ for all $i\in \N$ and for all $\epsilon > 0$.

theorem_like_statements.label: definition-5
theorem_like_statements.raw_latex:
[Heavy coefficient]
    For a function $f:G \rightarrow \C$ and a threshold $\tau>0$, we say that a coefficient $\widehat{f}(\alpha)$ (of the character $\chi_\alpha$) is \emph{$\tau$-heavy} if $|\widehat{f}(\alpha)|^2>\tau$.
theorem_like_statements.statement: [Heavy coefficient] For a function $f:G \rightarrow \C$ and a threshold $\tau>0$, we say that a coefficient $\widehat{f}(\alpha)$ (of the character $\chi_\alpha$) is \emph{$\tau$-heavy} if $|\widehat{f}(\alpha)|^2>\tau$.

theorem_like_statements.label: Chernoff
theorem_like_statements.raw_latex:
[Chernoff] \label{Chernoff}
    Let $A$ be a set of complex numbers such that $|x|\leq M$ for all $x\in A$. Let $x_i\in A$ be chosen independently and uniformly at randomly from $A$. Then
    \[
        \Pr\left[\left|\E_{x\in A}[x]-\frac{1}{m}\sum_{i=1}^mx_i\right| > \lambda \right]\leq 2 \mathrm{e}^{-\lambda^2 m / 2 M^2} .
    \]
theorem_like_statements.statement: [Chernoff] Let $A$ be a set of complex numbers such that $|x|\leq M$ for all $x\in A$. Let $x_i\in A$ be chosen independently and uniformly at randomly from $A$. Then \[ \Pr\left[\left|\E_{x\in A}[x]-\frac{1}{m}\sum_{i=1}^mx_i\right| > \lambda \right]\leq 2 \mathrm{e}^{-\lambda^2 m / 2 M^2} . \]

theorem_like_statements.label: thm_sft_algo
theorem_like_statements.statement: [{\cite[SFT algorithm]{AKA-thesis}}{\cite[Theorem 5]{AGS}}] Let $G$ be an abelian group represented by a set of generators of known orders. There is a learning algorithm that, given query access to a function $f: G \rightarrow \C$, a threshold $\tau > 0$ and $\delta > 0$, outputs a list $L$ of size at most $2\|f\|_2^2 / \tau$ such that \begin{itemize} \item $L$ contains all the $\tau$-heavy Fourier coefficients of $f$ with probability at least $1-\delta$; \item $L$ does not contain coefficients that are not $(\tau/2)$-heavy with probability at least $1-\delta$. \end{itemize} The algorithm runs in polynomial time in $\log\left(|G|\right)$, $\| f \|_\infty^2 / \tau$ and $\log\left(\frac{1}{\delta}\right)$.

theorem_like_statements.label: thm:LS
theorem_like_statements.raw_latex:
[{\cite[Theorem 1.1]{LS}}]\label{thm:LS}
    Let $\{n_k\}_{k\in\N},\{m_k\}_{k\in\N}$ two sequences of positive integers with $m_k \geq n_k/2$ for every $k\in\N$.
    Let $Q\in \R[x]$ be a polynomial.
    Let $\{f_k:\Z_{n_k}\to\C\}_{k\in\N}$ be a concentrated family of functions such that $\|f_k\|_2^2 \leq Q(\log(n_k))$ for all $k\in \N$.
    Then $\{\widetilde{f_k}:\Z_{m_k}\to\C\}_{k\in\N}$ is a concentrated family of functions.
theorem_like_statements.statement: [{\cite[Theorem 1.1]{LS}}] Let $\{n_k\}_{k\in\N},\{m_k\}_{k\in\N}$ two sequences of positive integers with $m_k \geq n_k/2$ for every $k\in\N$. Let $Q\in \R[x]$ be a polynomial. Let $\{f_k:\Z_{n_k}\to\C\}_{k\in\N}$ be a concentrated family of functions such that $\|f_k\|_2^2 \leq Q(\log(n_k))$ for all $k\in \N$. Then $\{\widetilde{f_k}:\Z_{m_k}\to\C\}_{k\in\N}$ is a concentrated family of functions.

theorem_like_statements.label: thm:mod-switch
theorem_like_statements.statement: [{\cite[Theorem 6.1]{LS}}] Consider a family of functions $\famly = \{ f_{2^k}:\Z_{2^k} \to \C \}_{k\in\N}$ and define the family $\famly' = \{ f_n:\Z_n \to \C \}_{n\in\N}$, where for each $2^{k-1} < n \leq 2^k$ we let $f_n(x) := f_{2^k}(x)$ for every $x\in\Z_n$. If $\famly$ is concentrated then $\famly'$ is concentrated.

theorem_like_statements.label: lemma-11
theorem_like_statements.raw_latex:
[{\cite[Lemma 6.2]{LS}}]
    Let $k \in \N$ and $0 \le i < k$.
    Define $\bit_i : \Z_{2^k} \to \{-1,1\}$ by
    $\bit_i(x) = (-1)^{x_i}$ where $x = \sum_{j=0}^{k-1} x_j 2^j$ and $x_j \in \{ 0,1 \}$.
    Let $\alpha \in \Z_{2^k}$.
    Then $\widehat{\bit_i}(\alpha) = 0$ unless $\alpha$ is an odd multiple of $2^{k-i-1}$ in which case $| \widehat{\bit_i}(\alpha)| = O( 2^{k-i}/|\alpha|_{2^k} )$.
theorem_like_statements.statement: [{\cite[Lemma 6.2]{LS}}] Let $k \in \N$ and $0 \le i < k$. Define $\bit_i : \Z_{2^k} \to \{-1,1\}$ by $\bit_i(x) = (-1)^{x_i}$ where $x = \sum_{j=0}^{k-1} x_j 2^j$ and $x_j \in \{ 0,1 \}$. Let $\alpha \in \Z_{2^k}$. Then $\widehat{\bit_i}(\alpha) = 0$ unless $\alpha$ is an odd multiple of $2^{k-i-1}$ in which case $| \widehat{\bit_i}(\alpha)| = O( 2^{k-i}/|\alpha|_{2^k} )$.

theorem_like_statements.label: HNP_def
theorem_like_statements.statement: [Hidden number problem] Let $(G,\cdot)$ be a group, let $s \neq 0$ be a secret (unknown) element of $G$ and let $f$ be a function defined over $G$. Find $s$ using oracle access to the function $f_s (x) := f(s \cdot x)$.

theorem_like_statements.label: HNP_thm
theorem_like_statements.statement: [\cite{AGS}] Let $f: \Z_N \to \{-1,1\}$ be a function with a $\tau$-heavy Fourier coefficient $\alpha\in\Z^*_N$ for $\tau^{-1}=poly(\log|G|)$. Then, the chosen-multiplier hidden number problem in $\Z_N$ with $s \in \Z^*_N$ and the function $f$ can be solved in polynomial time.

theorem_like_statements.label: cor_HNP-CM
theorem_like_statements.raw_latex:
[\cite{AGS}]
%\label{cor_HNP-CM}
%For any concentrated function $f: \Z_N \rightarrow \{-1,1\}$, there exists an algorithm that solves CM-HNP in $\Z^*_N$ in polynomial time, where the queries are made non-adaptively.
%
theorem_like_statements.statement: [\cite{AGS}] % %For any concentrated function $f: \Z_N \rightarrow \{-1,1\}$, there exists an algorithm that solves CM-HNP in $\Z^*_N$ in polynomial time, where the queries are made non-adaptively. %

theorem_like_statements.label: MVHNP_def
theorem_like_statements.statement: [Multivariate hidden number problem] Let $R$ be a ring, let $\textbf{s}=(s_1,\dots,s_m)\ne (0,\dots,0)$ be a secret (unknown) element in $R^m$ and let $f$ be a function defined over $R$. Find $\textbf{s}$ using oracle access to the function $f_\textbf{s}(\textbf{x}) := f (\textbf{s} \cdot \textbf{x}) = f (s_1 x_1 + \cdots + s_m x_m$).

theorem_like_statements.label: MVHNP_thm
theorem_like_statements.statement: [\cite{MVHNP}] Let $f: \Z_p \to \{-1,1\}$ be a function with a $\tau$-heavy Fourier coefficient $\alpha\in\Z^*_p$ for $\tau^{-1}=poly(\log|G|)$. Then, the chosen-multiplier multivariate hidden number problem in $\Z_p^m$ with the function $f$ can be solved in polynomial time.

theorem_like_statements.label: lem_character_sum
theorem_like_statements.statement: Let $\varphi = \frac{g}{h}$ be a non-constant quotient of two univariate polynomials with coefficients in $\F_{p^m}$, let $Z_h$ denote the set of roots of $h$, and let $\chi$ be a non-trivial additive character of $\F_{p^m}$. The following bound holds \[ \left| \sum_{x \in \F_{p^m} \setminus Z_h} \chi\left(\varphi(x)\right) \right| \leq \left(\max\left\{\deg(g), \deg(h)\right\} + u - 2 \right) \sqrt{p^m} + \delta \enspace, \] where \begin{displaymath} (u,\delta) = \left\{ \begin{array}{l l} (v,1) & \quad if \ \deg(g) \leq \deg(h),\\ (v+1,0) & \quad if \ \deg(g)>\deg(h), \end{array} \right . \end{displaymath} and $v$ is the number of distinct roots of $h$ in the algebraic closure of $\F_{p^m}$.

theorem_like_statements.label: lem_character_sum
theorem_like_statements.statement: Let $q$ be prime. For any polynomials $f,g \in \F_q[x]$ such that the rational function $h = \frac{f}{g}$ is not constant in $\F_q$, the following bound holds \[ \Bigg| \sum_{\lambda \in \F_p}{}^{*} \omega_q^{h(\lambda)} \Bigg| \leq (\max\{\deg(f), \deg(g)\} + u -2) \sqrt{q} + \delta \,, \] where $\sum^*$ means that the summation is taken over all $\lambda \in \F_q$ which are not poles of $h$ and \begin{displaymath} (u,\delta) = \left\{ \begin{array}{l l} (v,1) & \quad if \ \deg(f) \leq \deg(g),\\ (v+1,0) & \quad if \ \deg(f)>\deg(g), \end{array} \right . \end{displaymath} and $v$ is the number of distinct zeros of $g$ in the algebraic closure of $\F_q$.

theorem_like_statements.label: prop_limit
theorem_like_statements.raw_latex:
\label{prop_limit}
    Let $q$ be a sufficiently large prime.
    Let $f$ be a concentrated function on $\Z_q$ such that $\|f\|_2=1$ and $\widehat{f}(0) = 0$.
    Let $g, h \in \Z_q[x]$ be polynomials of degree bounded by $poly(\log(q))$ and let $Z_h$ be the set of zeroes of $h$.
    Define $\varphi(x)$ as above and suppose this function is non-constant.
    Let $\tau = 1/poly(\log(q))$. If $f \circ \varphi$ has any $\tau$-heavy Fourier coefficients then $\varphi(x) = ax + b$ for some $a, b \in \Z_q$.
theorem_like_statements.statement: Let $q$ be a sufficiently large prime. Let $f$ be a concentrated function on $\Z_q$ such that $\|f\|_2=1$ and $\widehat{f}(0) = 0$. Let $g, h \in \Z_q[x]$ be polynomials of degree bounded by $poly(\log(q))$ and let $Z_h$ be the set of zeroes of $h$. Define $\varphi(x)$ as above and suppose this function is non-constant. Let $\tau = 1/poly(\log(q))$. If $f \circ \varphi$ has any $\tau$-heavy Fourier coefficients then $\varphi(x) = ax + b$ for some $a, b \in \Z_q$.

theorem_like_statements.label: statement-20
theorem_like_statements.statement: theorem [ LS ] thm:LS Let \ n k\ k ,\ m k\ k two sequences of positive integers with m k n k/2 for every k .

title: Finding Significant Fourier Coefficients: Clarifications, Simplifications, Applications and Limitations

mechanism: correlated sample-pair localization; filtered hash-to-bins Fourier measurements; hash-based sparse Fourier localization
no_go_barrier: Unfortunately, the identification step alone increases the sampling complexity by O( N) per hash function, so unlike~ IK14a , here we cannot repeat this process using O( N) hash functions to ensure that each large coefficient is isolated by one of those functions.
open_question: Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) computes the DFT of a length N signal in time O(N N) , and finding a faster algorithm for DFT is a major open problem in theoretical computer science.
problem_family: sparse Fourier recovery; structured-query sparse Fourier recovery
proof_technique: \expect \bf E \prob \bf Pr \opt OPT gray rgb 0.5,0.5,0.5 \e \A A \E E \comment [1] gray [ #1 ] \grainofsalt [1] gray [ #1 ] \redospace \baselinestretch 1.5 \undospace \baselinestretch 1 theorem Theorem [section] lemma [theorem] Lemma claim [theorem] Claim corollary [theorem] Corollary fact [theorem] Fact definition [theorem] Definition remark [theorem] Remark conjecture [theorem] Conjecture proposition [theorem] Proposition proofof [1] \bf Proof of #1: hyperref bookmarksnumbered equation 1 \thee
reduction: Minimizing the sample complexity of acquiring a signal using Fourier measurements thus translates directly to reduction in the time the patient spends in the MRI machine~ MRICS while a scan is being taken.
reusable_abstraction: definition We access the signal x in Fourier domain via the function HashToBins x, , (H, a) , which evaluates the hashing H of residual signal x- at point a , i.e.

theorem_like_statements.label: definition-1
theorem_like_statements.raw_latex:
Suppose that $\Sigma^{-1}$ exists $\bmod~n$. For $a, q\in \nsq$ we define the
  permutation $P_{\Sigma, a, q}$ by $(P_{\Sigma, a, q}\hat x)_i=\hat x_{\Sigma^T(i-a)} \omega^{i^T\Sigma q}$.
theorem_like_statements.statement: Suppose that $\Sigma^{-1}$ exists $\bmod~n$. For $a, q\in \nsq$ we define the permutation $P_{\Sigma, a, q}$ by $(P_{\Sigma, a, q}\hat x)_i=\hat x_{\Sigma^T(i-a)} \omega^{i^T\Sigma q}$.

theorem_like_statements.label: lm:perm
theorem_like_statements.raw_latex:
\label{lm:perm}
$\F^{-1}({P_{\Sigma, a, q} \hat x})_{\pi_{\Sigma, q}(i)}=x_i \omega^{a^T \Sigma i}$
theorem_like_statements.statement: $\F^{-1}({P_{\Sigma, a, q} \hat x})_{\pi_{\Sigma, q}(i)}=x_i \omega^{a^T \Sigma i}$

theorem_like_statements.label: lm:filter-prop
theorem_like_statements.statement: [Lemma~3.1 in~\cite{IK14a}] One has {\bf (1)} $G_j\in [\frac1{(2\pi)^{\fc \cdot d}}, 1]$ for all $j\in \nsq$ such that $||j||_\infty\leq \frac{n}{2b}$ and {\bf (2)} $|G_j|\leq \left(\frac2{1+(b/n)||j||_\infty}\right)^{\fc}$ for all $j\in \nsq$ as long as $b\geq 3$ and {\bf (3)} $G_j\in [0, 1]$ for all $j$ as long as $F$ is even.

theorem_like_statements.label: lemma:limitedindependence
theorem_like_statements.statement: [Lemma~3.2 in~\cite{IK14a}] Let $i, j\in \nsq$. Let $\Sigma$ be uniformly random with odd determinant. Then for all $t\geq 0$ one has $ \Pr[||\Sigma(i-j)||_\infty \leq t] \leq 2(2t/n)^d$.

theorem_like_statements.label: definition-5
theorem_like_statements.raw_latex:
[Hashing $H=(\pi, B, F)$]
For a permutation $\pi=(\Sigma, q)$, parameters $b>1$, $B=b^d$ and $F$,  a {\em hashing} $H:=(\pi, B, F)$ is a function mapping a signal $x\in \C^{\nsq}$ to $B$ signals $H(x)=(u_s)_{s\in [b]^d}$, where $u_s\in \C^{\nsq}$ for each $s\in [b]^d$, such that for each $i\in \nsq$
$$
  u_{s, i}  = \sum_{j\in \nsq} G_{\pi(j)-(n/b)\cdot s}x_j \omega^{i^T \Sigma j}\in \C,
$$
where $G$ is a filter with $B$ buckets and sharpness $F$ constructed in Lemma~\ref{lm:filter-prop}.

theorem_like_statements.label: definition-6
theorem_like_statements.raw_latex:
[Measurement $m=m(x, H, a)$]
For a signal $x\in \C^{\nsq}$, a hashing $H=(\pi, B, F)$ and a parameter $a\in \nsq$, a {\em measurement} $m=m(x, H, a)\in \C^{[b]^d}$ is the $B$-dimensional complex valued vector of evaluations of a hashing $H(x)$ at $a\in \C^{\nsq}$, i.e. 
length $B$, indexed by $[b]^d$ and given by evaluating the hashing $H$ at $a\in \nsq$, i.e. for $s\in [b]^d$
$$
m_s  = \sum_{j\in \nsq} G_{\pi(j)-(n/b)\cdot s}x_j \omega^{a^T \Sigma j},
$$
where $G$ is a filter with $B$ buckets and sharpness $F$ constructed in Lemma~\ref{lm:filter-prop}.

sq$ 
$$\mu^2_{H, i}(x):=\abs{G_{o_i(i)}^{-1}}\sum_{j \in 
sq\setminus \{i\}} \abs{x_j}^2 \abs{G_{o_i(j)}}^2.$$
theorem_like_statements.statement:
For any $x\in \C^

theorem_like_statements.label: lm:hashing
theorem_like_statements.statement:
There exists a constant $C>0$ such that for any dimension $d\geq 1$, any integer $B\geq 1$, any $x, \chi\in \C^
sq, x':=x-\chi$, if $\Sigma\in \gl, a, q\in 
sq$ are selected uniformly at random, the following conditions hold. Let $\pi=(\Sigma, q)$, $H=(\pi, B, G)$, where $G$ is the filter with $B$ buckets and sharpness $F$ constructed in Lemma~ef{lm:filter-prop}, and let $u =\Call{HashToBins}{\hat x, \chi, (H, a)}$. Then if $ c\geq 2d, F=\Theta(d)$, for any $i\in 
sq$  egin{description} \item[(1)] For any $H$ one has $\max_{a\in 
sq} \abs{G_{o_i(i)}^{-1}\omega^{-a^T\Sigma i}u_{h(i)} - x'_i}\leq G_{o_i(i)}^{-1}\cdot \sum_{j\in S\setminus \{i\}} G_{o_i(j)} |x'_j|$. Furthermore, $\expect_H[G_{o_i(i)}^{-1}\cdot \sum_{j\in S\setminus \{i\}} G_{o_i(j)} |x'_j|]\leq \GS\cdot C^d ||x'||_1/B+N^{-\Omega(c)}$; \item[(2)] $\expect_H[\mu^2_{H, i}(x')] \leq \GSS\cdot C^d 
orm{2}{x'}^2/B$, \end{description} Furthermore,  egin{description} \item[(3)] for any hashing $H$, if $a$ is chosen uniformly at random from $
sq$, one has $$ \expect_{a}[\abs{G_{o_i(i)}^{-1}\omega^{-a^T\Sigma i}u_{h(i)} - x'_i}^2]\leq \mu^2_{H, i}(x')+N^{-\Omega(c)}. $$ \end{description} Here $c>0$ is an absolute constant that can be chosen arbitrarily large at the expense of a factor of $c^{O(d)}$ in runtime.

theorem_like_statements.label: def:isolated
theorem_like_statements.raw_latex:
[Isolated element]\label{def:isolated}
Let $H=(\pi, B, F)$, where $\pi=(\Sigma, q)$, $\Sigma\in \gl, q\in \nsq$. We say that an element $i\in \nsq$ is {\em isolated} under hashing $H$ {\em at scale $t$} if
$$
|\pi(S\setminus \{i\})\cap \B^\infty_{(n/b)\cdot h(i)}((n/b)\cdot 2^t)|\leq \GI\cdot \alpha^{d/2} 2^{(t+1)d}\cdot 2^{t}.
$$
We say that $i$ is simply {\em isolated} under hashing $H$ if it is isolated under $H$ at all scales $t\geq 0$.
theorem_like_statements.statement: [Isolated element] Let $H=(\pi, B, F)$, where $\pi=(\Sigma, q)$, $\Sigma\in \gl, q\in \nsq$. We say that an element $i\in \nsq$ is {\em isolated} under hashing $H$ {\em at scale $t$} if $$ |\pi(S\setminus \{i\})\cap \B^\infty_{(n/b)\cdot h(i)}((n/b)\cdot 2^t)|\leq \GI\cdot \alpha^{d/2} 2^{(t+1)d}\cdot 2^{t}. $$ We say that $i$ is simply {\em isolated} under hashing $H$ if it is isolated under $H$ at all scales $t\geq 0$.

theorem_like_statements.label: lm:isolated-pi
theorem_like_statements.raw_latex:
\label{lm:isolated-pi}
For any integer $k\geq 1$ and any $S\subseteq 
sq, |S|\leq 2k$, if  $B\geq \GT\cdot k/\alpha^d$ for $\alpha\in (0, 1)$ smaller than an absolute constant, $F\geq 2d$, and a hashing $H=(\pi, B, F)$ is chosen randomly (i.e. $\Sigma\in \gl, q\in 
sq$ are chosen uniformly at random, and $\pi=(\Sigma, q)$), then each $i\in 
sq$ is {\em isolated} under permutation $\pi$ with probability at least $1- rac1{2}\sqrt{\alpha}$.
theorem_like_statements.statement:
For any integer $k\geq 1$ and any $S\subseteq 
sq, |S|\leq 2k$, if $B\geq \GT\cdot k/\alpha^d$ for $\alpha\in (0, 1)$ smaller than an absolute constant, $F\geq 2d$, and a hashing $H=(\pi, B, F)$ is chosen randomly (i.e. $\Sigma\in \gl, q\in 
sq$ are chosen uniformly at random, and $\pi=(\Sigma, q)$), then each $i\in 
sq$ is {\em isolated} under permutation $\pi$ with probability at least $1- rac1{2}\sqrt{\alpha}$.

theorem_like_statements.label: lm:good-prob
theorem_like_statements.raw_latex:
\label{lm:good-prob}
For any integer $k\geq 1$, and any $S\subseteq \nsq, |S|\leq 2k$, if $B\geq \GT\cdot k/\alpha^d$ for $\alpha\in (0, 1)$ smaller than an absolute constant, $F\geq 2d$, $H_r=(\pi_r, B, F)$, $r=1,\ldots, r_{max}$ a sequence of random hashings, then every $i\in \nsq$ is isolated with respect to $S$ under at least $(1-\sqrt{\alpha})r_{max}$ hashings $H_r, r=1,\ldots, r_{max}$ with probability at least $1-2^{-\Omega(\sqrt{\alpha}r_{max})}$.
theorem_like_statements.statement: For any integer $k\geq 1$, and any $S\subseteq \nsq, |S|\leq 2k$, if $B\geq \GT\cdot k/\alpha^d$ for $\alpha\in (0, 1)$ smaller than an absolute constant, $F\geq 2d$, $H_r=(\pi_r, B, F)$, $r=1,\ldots, r_{max}$ a sequence of random hashings, then every $i\in \nsq$ is isolated with respect to $S$ under at least $(1-\sqrt{\alpha})r_{max}$ hashings $H_r, r=1,\ldots, r_{max}$ with probability at least $1-2^{-\Omega(\sqrt{\alpha}r_{max})}$.

theorem_like_statements.label: def:balance
theorem_like_statements.raw_latex:
[Balanced set of points]\label{def:balance}
For an integer $\Delta\geq 2$  we say that a (multi)set $\mathcal{Z}\subseteq \nsq$ is {\em $\Delta$-balanced} in coordinate $s\in [1:d]$ if for every $r=1,\ldots, \Delta-1$ at least $49/100$ fraction of elements in the set $\{\omega_{\Delta}^{r\cdot z_s}\}_{z\in \mathcal{Z}}$ belong to the left halfplane $\{u\in \C: \text{Re}(u)\leq 0\}$ in the complex plane, where $\omega_\Delta=e^{2\pi i/\Delta}$ is the $\Delta$-th root of unity.
theorem_like_statements.statement: [Balanced set of points] For an integer $\Delta\geq 2$ we say that a (multi)set $\mathcal{Z}\subseteq \nsq$ is {\em $\Delta$-balanced} in coordinate $s\in [1:d]$ if for every $r=1,\ldots, \Delta-1$ at least $49/100$ fraction of elements in the set $\{\omega_{\Delta}^{r\cdot z_s}\}_{z\in \mathcal{Z}}$ belong to the left halfplane $\{u\in \C: \text{Re}(u)\leq 0\}$ in the complex plane, where $\omega_\Delta=e^{2\pi i/\Delta}$ is the $\Delta$-th root of unity.

theorem_like_statements.label: thm:l1-res-loc
theorem_like_statements.raw_latex:
\label{thm:l1-res-loc}
For any constant $C'>0$ there exist absolute constants $C_1, C_2, C_3>0$ such that for any $x, \chi\in \C^N$, $x'=x-\chi$, any integer $k\geq 1$ and any $S\subseteq \nsq$ such that $||x_{\nsq\setminus S}||_\infty\leq C'\mu$, where $\mu=||x_{\nsq\setminus [k]}||_2/\sqrt{k}$, the following conditions hold if $||x'||_\infty/\mu=N^{O(1)}$.

Let $\pi_r=(\Sigma_r, q_r), r=1,\ldots, r_{max}$ denote permutations, and let $H_r=(\pi_r, B, F)$, $F\geq 2d, F=\Theta(d)$, where $B\geq \GT k/\alpha^d$ for $\alpha\in (0, 1)$ smaller than a constant. Let $S^*\subseteq S$ denote the set of elements that are not isolated with respect to at least a $\sqrt{\alpha}$ fraction of hashings $\{H_r\}$. Then if additionally for every $s\in [1:d]$ the sets $\A_r\star (\one, \mathbf{e}_s)$ are balanced in coordinate $s$ (as per Definition~\ref{def:balance}) for all $r=1,\ldots, r_{max}$, and $r_{max}, c_{max}\geq (C_1/\sqrt{\alpha})\log\log N$, then
$$
L:=\bigcup_{r=1}^{r_{max}}\textsc{LocateSignal}\left(\chi, k, \{m(\wh{x}, H_r, a\star (\one, \h))\}_{r=1, a\in \A_r, \h\in \H}^{r_{max}}\right)
$$
satisfies
$$
||x'_{S\setminus S^*\setminus L}||_1\leq (C_2\alpha)^{d/2} ||x'_S||_1+C_3^{d^2}(||\chi_{\nsq\setminus S}||_1+||x'_{S^*}||_1)+4\mu |S|.
$$

theorem_like_statements.label: lm:reduce-l1-norm
thm:l1snr
lm:const-snr
theorem_like_statements.raw_latex:
\label{lm:const-snr}
For any $\e>0$, $\hat x, \chi\in \C^N$, $x'=x-\chi$ and any integer $k\geq 1$ if $||x'_{[2k]}||_1\leq O(||x_{\nsq\setminus [k]}||_2\sqrt{k})$ and $||x'_{\nsq\setminus [2k]}||_2^2\leq ||x_{\nsq\setminus [k]}||_2^2$, the following conditions hold. If $||x||_\infty/\mu=N^{O(1)}$, then the output $\chi'$ of 
\Call{RecoverAtConstantSNR}{$\hat x, \chi, 2k, \e$} satisfies
$$
||x'-\chi'||^2_2\leq (1+O(\e))||x_{\nsq\setminus [k]}||_2^2
$$
with at least $99/100$ probability over its internal randomness. The sample complexity is $2^{O(d^2)}\frac1{\e}  k\log N$, and the runtime complexity is at most $2^{O(d^2)}\frac1{\e}  k \log^{d+1} N.$

theorem_like_statements.label: thm:main
theorem_like_statements.statement: For any $\e>0$, $x\in \C^\nsq$ and any integer $k\geq 1$, if $R^*\geq ||x||_\infty/\mu=N^{O(1)}$, $\mu^2=O(||x_{\nsq\setminus [k]}||_2^2/k)$ and $\alpha>0$ is smaller than an absolute constant, \textsc{SparseFFT}$(\hat x, k, \e, R^*, \mu)$ solves the $\ell_2/\ell_2$ sparse recovery problem using $2^{O(d^2)} (k\log N \log\log N+\frac1{\e}k\log N)$ samples and $2^{O(d^2)} \frac1{\e}k \log^{d+3} N$ time with at least $98/100$ success probability.

theorem_like_statements.label: lm:loc
theorem_like_statements.statement:
Let $H=(\pi, B, R)$ be a hashing, and let $\A\subseteq 
sq	imes 
sq$. Then for every $S\subseteq 
sq$ and for every $x, \chi\in \C^{
sq}$ and $x'=x-\chi$, the following conditions hold. Let $L$ denote the output of $$ \Call{LocateSignal}{\chi, H, \{m(\wh{x}, H, a\star (\one, \h))\}_{a\in \A, \h\in \H}}. $$ Then for any $i\in S$ such that $|x'_i|>N^{-\Omega(c)}$, if there exists $r\in [1:r_{max}]$ such that  egin{enumerate} \item $e^{head}_{i}(H, x')<|x'_i|/20$; \item $e^{tail}_{i}(H, \A\star (\one, \h), x')< |x'_i|/20$ for all $\h\in \H$; \item for every $s\in [1:d]$ the set $\A\star(\zero, \mathbf{e}_s)$ is balanced in coordinate $s$ (as per Definition~ef{def:balance}), \end{enumerate} then $i\in L$. The time taken by the invocation of 	extsc{LocateSignal} is $O(B\cdot \log^{d+1} N)$.

theorem_like_statements.label: cor:loc
theorem_like_statements.statement:
For any integer $r_{max}\geq 1$, for any sequence of $r_{max}$ hashings $H_r=(\pi_r, B, R), r\in [1:r_{max}]$ and evaluation points $\A_r\subseteq 
sq	imes 
sq$, for every $S\subseteq 
sq$ and for every $x, \chi\in \C^{
sq}, x':=x-\chi$, the following conditions hold. If for each $r\in [1:r_{max}]$ $L_r\subseteq 
sq$ denotes the output of \Call{LocateSignal}{$\wh{x}, \chi, H_r, \{m(\wh{x}, H_r, a\star (\one, \h))\}_{a\in \A_r, \h\in \H}$}, $L= igcup_{r=1}^{r_{max}} L_r$, and the sets $\A_r\star(\zero, \h)$ are balanced for all $\h\in \H$ and $r\in [1:r_{max}]$, then  egin{equation} ||x'_{S\setminus L}||_1\leq 20 ||e^{head}_S(\{H_r\}, x, \chi)||_1+20 ||e^{tail}_S(\{H_r, \A_r\}, x)||_1+|S|\cdot N^{-\Omega(c)}.	ag{*} \end{equation} Furthermore, every element $i\in S$ such that  egin{equation} |x'_i|>20 (e^{head}_i(\{H_r\}, x, \chi)+e^{tail}_i(\{H_r, \A_r\}, x))+N^{-\Omega(c)}	ag{**} \end{equation} belongs to $L$.

theorem_like_statements.label: lm:l1b-single-pi
theorem_like_statements.raw_latex:
\label{lm:l1b-single-pi}
Let $x, \chi\in \C^N, x'=x-\chi$. \defsk
Let $\pi=(\Sigma, q)$ be a permutation, let $H=(\pi, B, F), F\geq 2d$ be a hashing into $B$ buckets and filter $G$ with sharpness $F$. Let $S^*_H\subseteq S$ denote the set of elements $i\in S$ that are not isolated under $H$. Then one has, for $e^{head}$ defined with respect to $S$, 
$$
||e^{head}_{S\setminus S^*_H}(H, x, \chi)||_1\leq 2^{O(d)} \alpha^{d/2} ||x'_{S\setminus S^*_H}||_1+\GS \cdot 2^{O(d)} (||x'_{S^*}||_1+||\chi_{\nsq\setminus S}||_1).
$$
Furthermore, if $\chi_{\nsq\setminus S}=0$ and $S^*_H=\emptyset$, then one has $||e^{head}_{S}(H, x, \chi)||_\infty\leq 2^{O(d)}\alpha^{d/2} ||x'_S||_\infty$.
theorem_like_statements.statement: Let $x, \chi\in \C^N, x'=x-\chi$. \defsk Let $\pi=(\Sigma, q)$ be a permutation, let $H=(\pi, B, F), F\geq 2d$ be a hashing into $B$ buckets and filter $G$ with sharpness $F$. Let $S^*_H\subseteq S$ denote the set of elements $i\in S$ that are not isolated under $H$. Then one has, for $e^{head}$ defined with respect to $S$, $$ ||e^{head}_{S\setminus S^*_H}(H, x, \chi)||_1\leq 2^{O(d)} \alpha^{d/2} ||x'_{S\setminus S^*_H}||_1+\GS \cdot 2^{O(d)} (||x'_{S^*}||_1+||\chi_{\nsq\setminus S}||_1). $$ Furthermore, if $\chi_{\nsq\setminus S}=0$ and $S^*_H=\emptyset$, then one has $||e^{head}_{S}(H, x, \chi)||_\infty\leq 2^{O(d)}\alpha^{d/2} ||x'_S||_\infty$.

title: Sparse Fourier Transform in Any Constant Dimension with Nearly-Optimal Sample Complexity in Sublinear Time
